docs.title: MeasFlow
docs.text:
Prototype Stage

This project is in prototype stage. The API, file format, and feature set may still change significantly. Not intended for production use.

Open, high-performance measurement data format with multi-language support

Simple like TDMS, powerful like MDF4

Get Started View on GitHub

docs.title: Why MeasFlow?
docs.text:
• High Performance

    Streaming-first architecture with zero-copy reads and memory-mapped I/O

  • Open & Free

    MIT License with minimal dependencies - no vendor lock-in

  • Multi-Language

    Native support for C#, Python, and C with consistent APIs

  • Automotive Ready

    First-class support for CAN, CAN-FD, LIN, FlexRay, and Ethernet bus data

  • Efficient Compression

    Optional LZ4/Zstd compression for reduced storage

  • Instant Statistics

    Min/Max/Mean/StdDev computed during write, available instantly on read

docs.title: Comparison with Existing Formats
docs.text: Format Limitations TDMS (NI) Proprietary, limited bus data support HDF5 Complex C-based API, poor .NET interop MDF4 (ASAM) Extremely complex spec, restricted tooling MeasFlow Clean, open alternative with modern architecture

docs.title: Quick Example
Core Capabilities
docs.text:
• Streaming write with incremental flush
  • Typed channels: int8..uint64, float32/64, bool, timestamp
  • Properties at file, group, and channel level
  • Nanosecond-precision timestamps (MeasTimestamp)
  • Inline channel statistics (Min/Max/Mean/StdDev) — computed during write, available instantly on read

docs.title: Bus Data (MDF4-compatible)
docs.text:
• Bus types: CAN 2.0A/B, CAN-FD, LIN, FlexRay, Ethernet, MOST
  • Polymorphic frame definitions with bus-specific metadata
  • Signal definitions: start bit, bit length, Intel/Motorola byte order, factor/offset, value descriptions
  • PDU layer: AUTOSAR I-PDU with Container-PDU and Contained-PDU support
  • Multiplexing: MUX signals with nested multiplexing and range conditions
  • E2E Protection: AUTOSAR Profile 01-11, CRC/Counter positions
  • SecOC: Secure Onboard Communication with CMAC-AES/HMAC-SHA, freshness values, key management
  • Signal decoding: group.DecodeSignal("RPM") extracts values directly from raw frames

docs.title: License
docs.text: MIT — see LICENSE

docs.title: Benchmarks
docs.text: Performance comparison benchmarks are included in each language binding:

docs.title: C# Benchmarks
docs.text:
cd csharp/benchmarks/MeasFlow.Benchmarks
dotnet run -c Release -- --filter "*FormatComparison*"   # MeasFlow vs HDF5
dotnet run -c Release -- --filter "*CrossLanguage*"      # Cross-language

docs.title: Python Benchmarks
docs.text:
cd python
pip install h5py                              # for HDF5 comparison
python benchmarks/format_comparison.py        # MeasFlow vs HDF5
python benchmarks/cross_language.py           # Cross-language

docs.title: C Benchmarks
docs.text:
cd c
cmake -B build -DMEAS_BUILD_BENCHMARKS=ON -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release
cmake --build build
./build/bench_format_comparison
./build/bench_cross_language

Avalonia-based GUI for viewing measurement files:

cd csharp/tools/MeasFlow.Viewer
dotnet run

docs.title: Demo Generator (C#)

cd csharp/tools/MeasFlow.DemoGenerator

docs.title: Standards and Formats
docs.text:
• ASAM MDF - Measurement Data Format (MDF4)
  • HDF5 - Hierarchical Data Format
  • TDMS - National Instruments format

docs.title: AUTOSAR
docs.text:
• AUTOSAR - Automotive Open System Architecture
  • E2E Protection - End-to-End communication protection
  • SecOC - Secure Onboard Communication

docs.title: Project Structure
docs.text:
MeasFlow/
├── csharp/                       # C# implementation
│   ├── src/MeasFlow/             # Core library
│   │   ├── Bus/                  # Bus data model
│   │   └── Format/               # Binary serialization
│   ├── tests/MeasFlow.Tests/     # Test suite
│   ├── samples/QuickStart/       # Sample code
│   ├── benchmarks/               # Performance tests
│   └── tools/                    # Viewer and utilities
├── python/                       # Python implementation
│   ├── measflow/                 # Package source
│   ├── tests/                    # Test suite
│   ├── quickstart/               # Sample code
│   └── benchmarks/               # Performance tests
├── c/                            # C implementation
│   ├── measflow.c/.h             # Single-file library
│   ├── tests/                    # Test suite
│   ├── quickstart/               # Sample code
│   └── benchmarks/               # Performance tests
├── docs/                         # This documentation
├── SPECIFICATION.md              # Binary format spec
├── CONCEPT.md                    # Design document
└── README.md                     # Project overview

docs.title: Complete Specification
docs.text:
The complete binary format specification is maintained in the repository.

View SPECIFICATION.md on GitHub →

docs.title: What's Included
docs.text:
The specification document provides:

  • Complete binary layout with byte offsets and field descriptions
  • Data type encoding for all supported types
  • Segment structure for metadata and data segments
  • Bus metadata format for CAN, LIN, FlexRay, Ethernet
  • Compression format and algorithms
  • Conformance requirements and validation rules
  • Versioning strategy for backward compatibility
  • Extension points for future additions

docs.title: Quick Reference
docs.text: For a high-level overview of the file format, see the File Format Guide.

docs.title: Implementation Notes
docs.text:
The specification is designed to be:

  • Language-agnostic: Implementable in any programming language
  • Self-contained: No external dependencies required
  • Testable: Clear validation rules for conformance testing
  • Extensible: Reserved fields for backward-compatible additions

docs.title: Contributing
docs.text:
Found an issue or ambiguity in the specification?

Open an issue on GitHub →

docs.title: C API Reference
docs.text: Comprehensive API reference for the C binding.

docs.title: meas_writer_open
docs.text:
Create a new measurement file writer.

meas_writer_t* meas_writer_open(const char* path);

Parameters:

  • path - File path for the new measurement file

Returns: meas_writer_t* pointer or NULL on error

Example:

meas_writer_t* writer = meas_writer_open("data.meas");
if (!writer) {
    fprintf(stderr, "Failed to create writer\n");
    return 1;
}

docs.title: meas_writer_add_group
docs.text:
Add a new group to organize channels.

meas_group_t* meas_writer_add_group(meas_writer_t* writer, const char* name);

Parameters:

  • writer - Writer instance
  • name - Group name

Returns: meas_group_t* pointer or NULL on error

Example:

meas_group_t* group = meas_writer_add_group(writer, "Sensors");

docs.title: meas_group_add_channel
docs.text:
Add a channel with data type.

meas_channel_t* meas_group_add_channel(meas_group_t* group, const char* name, meas_data_type_t type);

Parameters:

  • group - Group instance
  • name - Channel name
  • type - Data type constant (e.g., MEAS_FLOAT32)

Returns: meas_channel_t* pointer or NULL on error

Example:

meas_channel_t* temp = meas_group_add_channel(group, "Temperature", MEAS_FLOAT32);
meas_channel_t* count = meas_group_add_channel(group, "Count", MEAS_INT32);

docs.title: meas_channel_write_*
docs.text:
Write data to the channel (type-specific functions).

int meas_channel_write_f32(meas_channel_t* ch, const float* data, size_t count);
int meas_channel_write_f64(meas_channel_t* ch, const double* data, size_t count);
int meas_channel_write_i32(meas_channel_t* ch, const int32_t* data, size_t count);
int meas_channel_write_i64(meas_channel_t* ch, const int64_t* data, size_t count);
// ... and more for other types

Parameters:

  • ch - Channel instance
  • data - Pointer to data array
  • count - Number of elements to write

Returns: 0 on success, non-zero on error

Example:

float values[] = {25.5f, 26.0f, 25.8f};
meas_channel_write_f32(temp, values, 3);

docs.title: meas_channel_write_frame
docs.text:
Write variable-length binary frame.

int meas_channel_write_frame(meas_channel_t* ch, const void* data, size_t len);

Parameters:

  • ch - Channel instance (must be MEAS_BINARY type)
  • data - Pointer to frame data
  • len - Frame length in bytes

Returns: 0 on success, non-zero on error

Example:

uint8_t frame[] = {0x12, 0x34, 0x56, 0x78};
meas_channel_write_frame(bus_channel, frame, sizeof(frame));

docs.title: meas_writer_flush
docs.text:
Flush current data to a new segment.

void meas_writer_flush(meas_writer_t* writer);

Creates a new data segment and clears the in-memory buffer.

Example:

while (recording) {
    float batch[10000];
    read_from_sensor(batch, 10000);
    meas_channel_write_f32(temp, batch, 10000);
    meas_writer_flush(writer);
}

docs.title: meas_writer_close
docs.text:
Finalize and close the file.

void meas_writer_close(meas_writer_t* writer);

Writes any remaining data, updates header, and frees resources.

Example:

meas_writer_close(writer);

docs.title: meas_reader_open
docs.text:
Open an existing measurement file.

meas_reader_t* meas_reader_open(const char* path);

Parameters:

  • path - Path to existing measurement file

Returns: meas_reader_t* pointer or NULL on error

Example:

meas_reader_t* reader = meas_reader_open("data.meas");
if (!reader) {
    fprintf(stderr, "Failed to open file\n");
    return 1;
}

docs.title: meas_reader_group_by_name
docs.text:
Get group by name.

const meas_group_data_t* meas_reader_group_by_name(meas_reader_t* reader, const char* name);

Parameters:

  • reader - Reader instance
  • name - Group name

Returns: const meas_group_data_t* pointer or NULL if not found

Example:

const meas_group_data_t* group = meas_reader_group_by_name(reader, "Sensors");

docs.title: meas_group_channel_by_name
docs.text:
Get channel by name.

const meas_channel_data_t* meas_group_channel_by_name(const meas_group_data_t* group, const char* name);

Parameters:

  • group - Group instance
  • name - Channel name

Returns: const meas_channel_data_t* pointer or NULL if not found

Example:

const meas_channel_data_t* ch = meas_group_channel_by_name(group, "Temperature");

docs.title: meas_channel_read_*
docs.text:
Read data into buffer (type-specific functions).

size_t meas_channel_read_f32(const meas_channel_data_t* ch, float* buf, size_t count);
size_t meas_channel_read_f64(const meas_channel_data_t* ch, double* buf, size_t count);
size_t meas_channel_read_i32(const meas_channel_data_t* ch, int32_t* buf, size_t count);
// ... and more for other types

Parameters:

  • ch - Channel instance
  • buf - Buffer to read into
  • count - Maximum number of elements to read

Returns: Number of elements actually read

Example:

float data[1000];
size_t read = meas_channel_read_f32(ch, data, 1000);
printf("Read %zu values\n", read);

docs.title: meas_channel_next_frame
docs.text:
Iterate through binary frames.

int meas_channel_next_frame(const meas_channel_data_t* ch, meas_frame_iter_t* state, const void** data, size_t* len);

Parameters:

  • ch - Channel instance (must be MEAS_BINARY type)
  • state - Iterator state (initialize to 0)
  • data - Output pointer to frame data
  • len - Output frame length

Returns: 1 if frame read, 0 if no more frames, -1 on error

Example:

meas_frame_iter_t iter = 0;
const void* frame_data;
size_t frame_len;

while (meas_channel_next_frame(ch, &iter, &frame_data, &frame_len) == 1) {
    printf("Frame: %zu bytes\n", frame_len);
    // Process frame_data
}

docs.title: Channel Data Structure
docs.text:
Channel information is available through meas_channel_data_t:

typedef struct {
    const char* name;
    meas_data_type_t data_type;
    uint64_t sample_count;
    bool has_stats;
    meas_statistics_t stats;
    // ... other fields
} meas_channel_data_t;

Fields:

  • name - Channel name
  • data_type - Data type constant
  • sample_count - Total number of samples
  • has_stats - Whether statistics are available
  • stats - Pre-computed statistics

Example:

if (ch->has_stats) {
    printf("Min: %.2f, Max: %.2f\n", ch->stats.min, ch->stats.max);
    printf("Mean: %.2f, StdDev: %.2f\n", ch->stats.mean, ch->stats.std_dev);
}

printf("Total samples: %llu\n", (unsigned long long)ch->sample_count);

docs.title: meas_reader_close
docs.text:
Close the reader and free resources.

void meas_reader_close(meas_reader_t* reader);

Example:

meas_reader_close(reader);

docs.title: Data Type Constants
docs.text: Constant C Type Bytes MEAS_INT8 int8_t 1 MEAS_INT16 int16_t 2 MEAS_INT32 int32_t 4 MEAS_INT64 int64_t 8 MEAS_UINT8 uint8_t 1 MEAS_UINT16 uint16_t 2 MEAS_UINT32 uint32_t 4 MEAS_UINT64 uint64_t 8 MEAS_FLOAT32 float 4 MEAS_FLOAT64 double 8 MEAS_BOOL uint8_t (0/1) 1 MEAS_TIMESTAMP int64_t (nanoseconds) 8 MEAS_BINARY Variable-length frames —

docs.title: Error Handling
docs.text:
Always check return values:

meas_reader_t* r = meas_reader_open("data.meas");
if (!r) {
    fprintf(stderr, "Failed to open file\n");
    return 1;
}

const meas_group_data_t* g = meas_reader_group_by_name(r, "Motor");
if (!g) {
    fprintf(stderr, "Group 'Motor' not found\n");
    meas_reader_close(r);
    return 1;
}

const meas_channel_data_t* rpm = meas_group_channel_by_name(g, "RPM");
if (rpm && rpm->has_stats) {
    printf("RPM range: %.1f - %.1f\n", rpm->stats.min, rpm->stats.max);
}

meas_reader_close(r);

docs.title: Streaming Write
docs.text:
Record continuous data with bounded memory:

meas_writer_t* w = meas_writer_open("recording.meas");
meas_group_t* g = meas_writer_add_group(w, "Sensors");
meas_channel_t* temp = meas_group_add_channel(g, "Temperature", MEAS_FLOAT32);

while (recording) {
    float batch[10000];
    read_from_sensor(batch, 10000);
    meas_channel_write_f32(temp, batch, 10000);
    meas_writer_flush(w);
}

meas_writer_close(w);

docs.title: Read All Data
docs.text:
Read complete channel:

const meas_channel_data_t* ch = /* ... */;

// Allocate buffer
float* data = malloc(ch->sample_count * sizeof(float));

// Read all
size_t read = meas_channel_read_f32(ch, data, ch->sample_count);

// Process
for (size_t i = 0; i < read; i++) {
    printf("%.2f\n", data[i]);
}

free(data);

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• C# API Reference
  • Python API Reference
  • Streaming Architecture
  • Installation

docs.title: C# API Reference
docs.text: Comprehensive API reference for the C# / .NET binding.

docs.title: MeasFile.CreateWriter
docs.text:
Create a new measurement file writer.

public static MeasFileWriter CreateWriter(string path)

Parameters:

  • path - File path for the new measurement file

Returns: MeasFileWriter instance (implements IDisposable)

Example:

using var writer = MeasFile.CreateWriter("data.meas");

docs.title: MeasFileWriter.AddGroup
docs.text:
Add a new group to organize channels.

public MeasGroupWriter AddGroup(string name)

Parameters:

  • name - Group name

Returns: MeasGroupWriter instance

Example:

var group = writer.AddGroup("Sensors");

docs.title: MeasGroupWriter.AddChannel\<T>
docs.text:
Add a typed channel to the group.

public MeasChannelWriter<T> AddChannel<T>(string name)

Type Parameters:

  • T - Data type (float, double, int, long, bool, etc.)

Parameters:

  • name - Channel name

Returns: MeasChannelWriter<T> instance

Example:

var temp = group.AddChannel<float>("Temperature");
var pressure = group.AddChannel<double>("Pressure");
var count = group.AddChannel<int>("Count");

docs.title: MeasChannelWriter.Write
docs.text:
Write data to the channel.

public void Write(T value)
public void Write(Span<T> values)
public void Write(ReadOnlySpan<T> values)

Parameters:

  • value - Single value to write
  • values - Span of values for efficient batch writing (zero-copy)

Example:

// Single value
temp.Write(25.5f);

// Multiple values (efficient)
float[] batch = new float[10000];
temp.Write(batch.AsSpan());

docs.title: MeasFileWriter.Flush
docs.text:
Flush current data to a new segment on disk.

public void Flush()

Creates a new data segment and clears the in-memory buffer. Use this periodically during streaming writes to keep memory usage bounded.

Example:

while (recording)
{
    float[] batch = ReadSensorData();
    temp.Write(batch.AsSpan());
    writer.Flush();  // Write segment, clear buffer
}

docs.title: MeasFileWriter.Dispose
docs.text:
Finalize and close the file.

public void Dispose()

Automatically called when using using statement. Writes any remaining data and updates file header.

docs.title: MeasFile.OpenRead
docs.text:
Open an existing measurement file for reading.

public static MeasFileReader OpenRead(string path)

Parameters:

  • path - Path to existing measurement file

Returns: MeasFileReader instance (implements IDisposable)

Example:

using var reader = MeasFile.OpenRead("data.meas");

docs.title: MeasFileReader Indexer
docs.text:
Access a group by name.

public MeasGroupReader this[string groupName] { get; }

Parameters:

  • groupName - Group name

Returns: MeasGroupReader instance

Example:

var group = reader["Sensors"];

docs.title: MeasGroupReader Indexer
docs.text:
Access a channel by name.

public MeasChannelReader this[string channelName] { get; }

Parameters:

  • channelName - Channel name

Returns: MeasChannelReader instance

Example:

var channel = group["Temperature"];
// Or chained:
var channel = reader["Sensors"]["Temperature"];

docs.title: MeasChannelReader.ReadAll\<T>
docs.text:
Read all samples as ReadOnlySpan<T>.

public ReadOnlySpan<T> ReadAll<T>()

Type Parameters:

  • T - Data type matching the channel type

Returns: ReadOnlySpan<T> containing all samples

Example:

var temp = reader["Sensors"]["Temperature"];
var data = temp.ReadAll<float>();

foreach (float value in data)
{
    Console.WriteLine(value);
}

docs.title: MeasChannelReader.ReadChunks\<T>
docs.text:
Iterate through data chunks (streaming).

public IEnumerable<ReadOnlySpan<T>> ReadChunks<T>()

Type Parameters:

  • T - Data type matching the channel type

Returns: Enumerable of ReadOnlySpan<T> chunks

Example:

// Only one chunk in memory at a time
foreach (var chunk in channel.ReadChunks<float>())
{
    Process(chunk);
}

docs.title: MeasChannelReader.Statistics
docs.text:
Get pre-computed statistics (instant, no I/O).

public MeasStatistics? Statistics { get; }

Returns: MeasStatistics instance or null if no statistics available

Properties:

  • Count - Number of samples
  • Min - Minimum value
  • Max - Maximum value
  • Mean - Average value
  • StdDev - Standard deviation

Example:

var stats = channel.Statistics;
if (stats != null)
{
    Console.WriteLine($"Min: {stats.Min}, Max: {stats.Max}");
    Console.WriteLine($"Mean: {stats.Mean}, StdDev: {stats.StdDev}");
}

docs.title: MeasChannelReader.Properties
docs.text:
Access channel metadata.

public IReadOnlyDictionary<string, object> Properties { get; }

Example:

if (channel.Properties.TryGetValue("unit", out var unit))
{
    Console.WriteLine($"Unit: {unit}");
}

docs.title: Bus Data API
docs.text: For automotive bus data (CAN, LIN, FlexRay, Ethernet).

docs.title: MeasFileWriter.AddBusGroup
docs.text:
Add a bus channel.

public MeasBusGroupWriter AddBusGroup(string name, BusConfig config)

Parameters:

  • name - Group name
  • config - Bus configuration (CAN, LIN, FlexRay, Ethernet)

Returns: MeasBusGroupWriter instance

Example:

var canConfig = new CanBusConfig { BusType = CanBusType.CanFd };
var busGroup = writer.AddBusGroup("CAN1", canConfig);

docs.title: MeasBusGroupWriter.DefineCanFrame
docs.text:
Define a CAN frame with signals.

public CanFrameDefinition DefineCanFrame(string name, uint id, byte length)

Parameters:

  • name - Frame name
  • id - CAN identifier
  • length - Payload length in bytes

Returns: CanFrameDefinition instance

Example:

var engineFrame = busGroup.DefineCanFrame("Engine", 0x123, 8);
engineFrame.Signals.Add(new SignalDefinition
{
    Name = "RPM",
    StartBit = 0,
    BitLength = 16,
    Factor = 0.25,
    Offset = 0,
    Unit = "rpm"
});

docs.title: MeasBusGroupWriter.WriteFrame
docs.text:
Write raw frame bytes with timestamp.

public void WriteFrame(MeasTimestamp timestamp, uint id, ReadOnlySpan<byte> data)

Parameters:

  • timestamp - Nanosecond-precision timestamp
  • id - Frame identifier
  • data - Frame payload bytes

Example:

byte[] payload = new byte[] { 0x12, 0x34, 0x56, 0x78 };
busGroup.WriteFrame(MeasTimestamp.Now, 0x123, payload);

docs.title: MeasBusGroupReader.DecodeSignal
docs.text:
Decode and extract signal values from frames.

public ReadOnlySpan<double> DecodeSignal(string signalName)

Parameters:

  • signalName - Signal name

Returns: Decoded signal values (with factor/offset applied)

Example:

var rpm = busGroup.DecodeSignal("RPM");
Console.WriteLine($"Average RPM: {rpm.ToArray().Average()}");

docs.title: Streaming Write
docs.text:
Record continuous data with bounded memory:

using var writer = MeasFile.CreateWriter("recording.meas");
var sensors = writer.AddGroup("Sensors");
var temp = sensors.AddChannel<float>("Temperature");

while (recording)
{
    float[] batch = ReadFromSensor(batchSize: 10_000);
    temp.Write(batch.AsSpan());
    writer.Flush();  // Write segment, clear buffer
}

docs.title: Streaming Read
docs.text:
Process large files chunk by chunk:

using var reader = MeasFile.OpenRead("recording.meas");
var channel = reader["Sensors"]["Temperature"];

foreach (var chunk in channel.ReadChunks<float>())
{
    // Only one chunk in memory at a time
    ProcessChunk(chunk);
}

docs.title: Instant Statistics
docs.text:
Get statistics without reading data:

using var reader = MeasFile.OpenRead("data.meas");
var channel = reader["Sensors"]["Temperature"];

var stats = channel.Statistics;
Console.WriteLine($"Range: {stats?.Min} - {stats?.Max}");
Console.WriteLine($"Mean: {stats?.Mean}");

// No data was read!

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• Python API Reference
  • C API Reference
  • Streaming Architecture
  • Bus Data Support

docs.title: Python API Reference
docs.text: Comprehensive API reference for the Python binding.

docs.title: MeasWriter
docs.text:
Create a new measurement file writer.

class MeasWriter:
    def __init__(self, path: str)

Parameters:

  • path - File path for the new measurement file

Context Manager: Use with with statement for automatic cleanup

Example:

with meas.Writer("data.meas") as writer:
    # Your code here
    pass

docs.title: add_group
docs.text:
Add a new group to organize channels.

def add_group(self, name: str) -> MeasGroupWriter

Parameters:

  • name - Group name

Returns: MeasGroupWriter instance

Example:

group = writer.add_group("Sensors")

docs.title: add_channel
docs.text:
Add a channel with data type.

def add_channel(self, name: str, dtype: MeasDataType) -> MeasChannelWriter

Parameters:

  • name - Channel name
  • dtype - Data type (e.g., MeasDataType.Float32)

Returns: MeasChannelWriter instance

Example:

temp = group.add_channel("Temperature", meas.DataType.Float32)
pressure = group.add_channel("Pressure", meas.DataType.Float64)
count = group.add_channel("Count", meas.DataType.Int32)

docs.title: write
docs.text:
Write data to the channel.

def write(self, value: Union[Any, List, np.ndarray])

Parameters:

  • value - Single value, list, or NumPy array

Example:

# Single value
temp.write(25.5)

# Multiple values
temp.write([25.5, 26.0, 25.8])

# NumPy array
import numpy as np
data = np.array([25.5, 26.0, 25.8], dtype=np.float32)
temp.write(data)

docs.title: flush
docs.text:
Flush current data to a new segment on disk.

def flush(self)

Creates a new data segment and clears the in-memory buffer. Use this periodically during streaming writes to keep memory usage bounded.

Example:

while recording:
    batch = read_sensor_data()
    temp.write(batch)
    writer.flush()  # Write segment, clear buffer

docs.title: properties
docs.text:
Set channel metadata.

channel.properties[key] = value

Example:

temp.properties["unit"] = "°C"
temp.properties["description"] = "Ambient temperature"

docs.title: MeasReader
docs.text:
Open an existing measurement file for reading.

class MeasReader:
    def __init__(self, path: str)

Parameters:

  • path - Path to existing measurement file

Context Manager: Use with with statement for automatic cleanup

Example:

with meas.Reader("data.meas") as reader:
    # Your code here
    pass

docs.title: Indexing
docs.text:
Access groups and channels by name.

reader[group_name]  # Access group
group[channel_name]  # Access channel

Example:

group = reader["Sensors"]
channel = group["Temperature"]
# Or chained:
channel = reader["Sensors"]["Temperature"]

docs.title: read_all
docs.text:
Read all samples as NumPy array.

def read_all(self) -> np.ndarray

Returns: NumPy array containing all samples

Example:

temp = reader["Sensors"]["Temperature"]
data = temp.read_all()

for value in data:
    print(value)

docs.title: read_timestamps
docs.text:
Read timestamp channel as array of datetime objects.

def read_timestamps(self) -> List[datetime]

Returns: List of datetime objects

Example:

timestamps = time_channel.read_timestamps()

docs.title: statistics
docs.text:
Get pre-computed statistics.

@property
def statistics(self) -> Optional[MeasStatistics]

Returns: MeasStatistics instance or None

Attributes:

  • count - Number of samples
  • min - Minimum value
  • max - Maximum value
  • mean - Average value
  • std_dev - Standard deviation

Example:

stats = channel.statistics
if stats:
    print(f"Min: {stats.min}, Max: {stats.max}")
    print(f"Mean: {stats.mean}, StdDev: {stats.std_dev}")

docs.title: properties
docs.text:
Access channel metadata.

channel.properties[key]

Example:

unit = channel.properties.get("unit", "")
print(f"Unit: {unit}")

docs.title: groups
docs.text:
Iterate over all groups in the file.

@property
def groups(self) -> List[MeasGroupReader]

Example:

for group in reader.groups:
    print(f"Group: {group.name}")
    for channel in group.channels:
        print(f"  Channel: {channel.name}")

docs.title: MeasDataType
docs.text:
Available data types for channels.

Type Description NumPy dtype MeasDataType.Int8 8-bit signed integer np.int8 MeasDataType.Int16 16-bit signed integer np.int16 MeasDataType.Int32 32-bit signed integer np.int32 MeasDataType.Int64 64-bit signed integer np.int64 MeasDataType.UInt8 8-bit unsigned integer np.uint8 MeasDataType.UInt16 16-bit unsigned integer np.uint16 MeasDataType.UInt32 32-bit unsigned integer np.uint32 MeasDataType.UInt64 64-bit unsigned integer np.uint64 MeasDataType.Float32 32-bit float np.float32 MeasDataType.Float64 64-bit float np.float64 MeasDataType.Bool Boolean np.bool_ MeasDataType.Timestamp Nanosecond timestamp np.int64 MeasDataType.Binary Variable-length binary bytes

docs.title: Streaming Write
docs.text:
Record continuous data with bounded memory:

with meas.Writer("recording.meas") as writer:
    sensors = writer.add_group("Sensors")
    temp = sensors.add_channel("Temperature", meas.DataType.Float32)

    while recording:
        batch = read_from_sensor(batch_size=10_000)
        temp.write(batch)
        writer.flush()  # Write segment, clear buffer

docs.title: Read and Analyze
docs.text:
Get statistics and process data with NumPy/Pandas:

with meas.Reader("data.meas") as reader:
    motor = reader["Motor"]

    # Get instant statistics (no data read)
    stats = motor["RPM"].statistics
    print(f"RPM: {stats.min}-{stats.max}, avg={stats.mean}")

    # Read all data for analysis
    rpm_data = motor["RPM"].read_all()
    temp_data = motor["Temperature"].read_all()

    # Process with NumPy/Pandas
    import numpy as np
    correlation = np.corrcoef(rpm_data, temp_data)[0, 1]
    print(f"Correlation: {correlation}")

docs.title: Iterate Groups and Channels
docs.text:
Explore file structure:

with meas.Reader("data.meas") as reader:
    for group in reader.groups:
        print(f"Group: {group.name}")
        for channel in group.channels:
            stats = channel.statistics
            print(f"  {channel.name}: {stats.count} samples")

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• C# API Reference
  • C API Reference
  • Streaming Architecture
  • Installation

docs.title: Installation
docs.text: MeasFlow provides native bindings for C#, Python, and C. Choose the installation method for your language.

docs.title: C# / .NET
docs.text:
MeasFlow is available on NuGet and requires .NET 10 or later.

dotnet add package MeasFlow

View on NuGet →

docs.title: Python
docs.text:
MeasFlow is available on PyPI and requires Python 3.10 or later.

pip install measflow

View on PyPI →

docs.title: C
docs.text:
MeasFlow is available via vcpkg (custom registry until official publication).

To use measflow via vcpkg, add a vcpkg-configuration.json to your project:

{
  "default-registry": null,
  "registries": [
    {
      "kind": "git",
      "repository": "https://github.com/vreitenbach/vcpkg-registry",
      "baseline": "d6473e3037973c8a5d465ef3fc1955b8e4f58557",
      "packages": ["measflow"]
    }
  ]
}

Then install:

vcpkg install measflow

View vcpkg registry →

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• C#: .NET 10 SDK
  • Python: Python 3.10+ with pip
  • C: C99 compiler, CMake 3.15+

docs.title: Clone Repository
docs.text:
git clone https://github.com/vreitenbach/MeasFlow.git
cd MeasFlow

docs.title: Build C
docs.text:
cd csharp
dotnet build
dotnet test

docs.title: Build Python
docs.text:
cd python
pip install -e .
pytest tests/

docs.title: Build C
docs.text:
cd c

# With compression (requires lz4 + zstd via vcpkg):
cmake -B build -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release \
  -DCMAKE_TOOLCHAIN_FILE=$VCPKG_INSTALLATION_ROOT/scripts/buildsystems/vcpkg.cmake
cmake --build build

# Or without compression:
cmake -B build -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release \
  -DMEAS_WITH_LZ4=OFF -DMEAS_WITH_ZSTD=OFF
cmake --build build

Note

CMAKE_BUILD_TYPE is for single-config generators (Ninja, Make). For multi-config generators (Visual Studio, Xcode), add --config Release to build commands and find binaries under <build-dir>/Release/.

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
• Quick Start Guide - Get started with your first measurement file
  • API Reference - Detailed API documentation
  • Examples - Sample code

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text: Get up and running with MeasFlow in minutes.

docs.title: Running the Quick Start Samples
docs.text:
Each language binding includes a runnable quickstart sample.

C#PythonC 
cd csharp/samples/QuickStart
dotnet run

The sample demonstrates: - Creating a measurement file - Adding groups and channels - Writing data with different types - Reading back the data - Accessing statistics

cd python
pip install -e .
python quickstart/quickstart.py

The sample demonstrates: - Creating a measurement file with context manager - Adding groups and channels - Writing arrays of data - Reading data as NumPy arrays - Accessing statistics

cd c

# Build quickstart
cmake -B build -DMEAS_BUILD_QUICKSTART=ON -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release \
  -DMEAS_WITH_LZ4=OFF -DMEAS_WITH_ZSTD=OFF
cmake --build build

# Run
./build/quickstart

The sample demonstrates: - Opening a writer - Creating groups and channels - Writing values - Closing the writer - Opening a reader - Reading values and statistics

docs.title: Your First Measurement File
docs.text:
Here's a minimal example to create and read a measurement file:

C#PythonC 
using MeasFlow;

// Write
using (var writer = MeasFile.CreateWriter("demo.meas"))
{
    var group = writer.AddGroup("Sensors");
    var temp = group.AddChannel<float>("Temperature");
    var pressure = group.AddChannel<double>("Pressure");

    temp.Write(25.5f);
    pressure.Write(101.3);

    writer.Flush();
}

// Read
using (var reader = MeasFile.OpenRead("demo.meas"))
{
    var temp = reader["Sensors"]["Temperature"];
    var data = temp.ReadAll<float>();
    var stats = temp.Statistics;

    Console.WriteLine($"Temperature: {data[0]}°C");
    Console.WriteLine($"Mean: {stats?.Mean}, StdDev: {stats?.StdDev}");
}

import measflow as meas

# Write
with meas.Writer("demo.meas") as writer:
    group = writer.add_group("Sensors")
    temp = group.add_channel("Temperature", meas.DataType.Float32)
    pressure = group.add_channel("Pressure", meas.DataType.Float64)

    temp.write([25.5])
    pressure.write([101.3])

    writer.flush()

# Read
with meas.Reader("demo.meas") as reader:
    temp = reader["Sensors"]["Temperature"]
    data = temp.read_all()
    stats = temp.statistics

    print(f"Temperature: {data[0]}°C")
    print(f"Mean: {stats.mean}, StdDev: {stats.std_dev}")

#include "measflow.h"
#include <stdio.h>

int main(void) {
    // Write
    meas_writer_t* writer = meas_writer_open("demo.meas");
    meas_group_t* group = meas_writer_add_group(writer, "Sensors");
    meas_channel_t* temp = meas_group_add_channel(group, "Temperature", MEAS_FLOAT32);
    meas_channel_t* pressure = meas_group_add_channel(group, "Pressure", MEAS_FLOAT64);

    float temp_val = 25.5f;
    double pressure_val = 101.3;

    meas_channel_write_f32(temp, &temp_val, 1);
    meas_channel_write_f64(pressure, &pressure_val, 1);

    meas_writer_flush(writer);
    meas_writer_close(writer);

    // Read
    meas_reader_t* reader = meas_reader_open("demo.meas");
    const meas_group_data_t* g = meas_reader_group_by_name(reader, "Sensors");
    const meas_channel_data_t* ch = meas_group_channel_by_name(g, "Temperature");

    float value;
    meas_channel_read_f32(ch, &value, 1);

    printf("Temperature: %.1f°C\n", value);

    if (ch->has_stats) {
        printf("Mean: %.1f, StdDev: %.1f\n", ch->stats.mean, ch->stats.std_dev);
    }

    meas_reader_close(reader);
    return 0;
}

docs.title: Streaming Data
docs.text:
MeasFlow excels at streaming large amounts of data without running out of memory:

C#PythonC 
using var writer = MeasFile.CreateWriter("recording.meas");
var sensors = writer.AddGroup("Sensors");
var temp = sensors.AddChannel<float>("Temperature");

// Record continuously
while (recording)
{
    float[] batch = ReadFromSensor(batchSize: 10_000);
    temp.Write(batch.AsSpan());
    writer.Flush();  // Flushes to disk, clears buffer
}
// Memory usage stays constant regardless of recording duration!

with meas.Writer("recording.meas") as writer:
    sensors = writer.add_group("Sensors")
    temp = sensors.add_channel("Temperature", meas.DataType.Float32)

    # Record continuously
    while recording:
        batch = read_from_sensor(batch_size=10_000)
        temp.write(batch)
        writer.flush()  # Flushes to disk, clears buffer
# Memory usage stays constant!

meas_writer_t* w = meas_writer_open("recording.meas");
meas_group_t* g = meas_writer_add_group(w, "Sensors");
meas_channel_t* temp = meas_group_add_channel(g, "Temperature", MEAS_FLOAT32);

// Record continuously
while (recording) {
    float batch[10000];
    read_from_sensor(batch, 10000);
    meas_channel_write_f32(temp, batch, 10000);
    meas_writer_flush(w);  // Flushes to disk, clears buffer
}
meas_writer_close(w);
// Memory usage stays constant!

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
• API Reference - Comprehensive API documentation
  • Streaming Architecture - Learn about the streaming design
  • Bus Data Support - Working with CAN, LIN, FlexRay, etc.
  • Specification - Complete binary format specification

docs.title: Bus Data Support
docs.text: MeasFlow provides first-class support for automotive bus data, compatible with MDF4 specifications.

docs.title: Supported Bus Types
docs.text:
• CAN 2.0A/B - Controller Area Network (11-bit and 29-bit identifiers)
  • CAN-FD - CAN with Flexible Data-Rate
  • LIN - Local Interconnect Network
  • FlexRay - High-speed automotive bus
  • Ethernet - Automotive Ethernet (100BASE-T1, 1000BASE-T1)
  • MOST - Media Oriented Systems Transport

docs.title: Architecture
docs.text:
BusChannelDefinition
 ├── BusConfig (CAN/CAN-FD/LIN/FlexRay/Ethernet/MOST)
 ├── Frames[]
 │    ├── FrameId, PayloadLength, Flags, Direction
 │    ├── Signals[] (direct signals when no PDU layer)
 │    └── Pdus[]
 │         ├── Signals[]
 │         ├── MultiplexConfig
 │         ├── E2EProtection
 │         ├── SecOcConfig
 │         └── ContainedPdus[] (AUTOSAR I-PDU Mux)
 └── ValueTables[]

docs.title: CAN Example
Basic Signal Properties
docs.text: Property Description Name Signal identifier StartBit Bit position in frame (0-based) BitLength Number of bits ByteOrder Intel (little-endian) or Motorola (big-endian) Factor Scaling factor (physical = raw × factor + offset) Offset Offset value Unit Physical unit (e.g., "rpm", "°C") ValueTable Enum mappings (0 → "Off", 1 → "On")

docs.title: Value Tables
docs.text:
Map raw values to human-readable strings:

var gearSignal = new SignalDefinition
{
    Name = "Gear",
    StartBit = 24,
    BitLength = 3,
    ValueTable = "GearStates"
};

busGroup.ValueTables.Add(new ValueTable
{
    Name = "GearStates",
    Entries = new Dictionary<long, string>
    {
        { 0, "Neutral" },
        { 1, "First" },
        { 2, "Second" },
        { 3, "Third" },
        { 4, "Fourth" },
        { 5, "Fifth" }
    }
});

docs.title: I-PDU Support
docs.text:
Protocol Data Units represent logical message groups:

var pdu = frame.AddPdu(new PduDefinition
{
    Name = "EnginePDU",
    StartByte = 0,
    Length = 8
});

pdu.Signals.Add(new SignalDefinition
{
    Name = "RPM",
    StartBit = 0,
    BitLength = 16,
    // ... signal properties
});

docs.title: Container-PDU
docs.text:
Multiple I-PDUs multiplexed in one frame:

var containerPdu = frame.AddPdu(new PduDefinition
{
    Name = "Container",
    StartByte = 0,
    Length = 64,
    IsContainer = true
});

containerPdu.ContainedPdus.Add(new ContainedPduDefinition
{
    Name = "PDU_A",
    HeaderId = 0x01,
    PayloadLength = 16
});

containerPdu.ContainedPdus.Add(new ContainedPduDefinition
{
    Name = "PDU_B",
    HeaderId = 0x02,
    PayloadLength = 32
});

docs.title: MUX Signals
docs.text:
Signals whose presence depends on multiplexer values:

var muxSignal = new SignalDefinition
{
    Name = "MuxSelector",
    StartBit = 0,
    BitLength = 8,
    IsMultiplexer = true
};

var signal_A = new SignalDefinition
{
    Name = "Value_A",
    StartBit = 8,
    BitLength = 16,
    MultiplexValue = 0  // Present when MuxSelector == 0
};

var signal_B = new SignalDefinition
{
    Name = "Value_B",
    StartBit = 8,
    BitLength = 16,
    MultiplexValue = 1  // Present when MuxSelector == 1
};

docs.title: Nested Multiplexing
docs.text:
Multiple levels of multiplexing:

pdu.MultiplexConfig = new MultiplexConfig
{
    Type = MultiplexType.ValueBased,
    SwitchSignal = "Level1_Mux",
    NestedLevels = new[]
    {
        new MultiplexLevel
        {
            SwitchSignal = "Level2_Mux",
            Conditions = new[] { 0, 1, 2 }
        }
    }
};

docs.title: E2E Protection
docs.text:
AUTOSAR End-to-End protection for data integrity:

pdu.E2EProtection = new E2EProtectionConfig
{
    Profile = E2EProfile.Profile01,
    DataId = 0x1234,
    CrcOffset = 0,    // CRC at byte 0
    CounterOffset = 1  // Counter at byte 1
};

// Supported profiles: 01-11

docs.title: SecOC (Secure Onboard Communication)
docs.text:
Authentication and encryption:

pdu.SecOcConfig = new SecOcConfig
{
    AuthAlgorithm = SecOcAuthAlgorithm.CmacAes,
    AuthTxBitLength = 64,
    AuthRxBitLength = 64,
    FreshnessValueLength = 64,
    FreshnessValueTxBitLength = 24,
    FreshnessValueRxBitLength = 24
};

docs.title: Signal Decoding
docs.text:
Extract physical values from raw frames:

C#Python 
using var reader = MeasFile.OpenRead("can_trace.meas");
var busGroup = reader["CAN1"];

// Decode single signal
var rpm = busGroup.DecodeSignal("RPM");
Console.WriteLine($"Average RPM: {rpm.ToArray().Average()}");

// Decode all signals in frame
var engineSignals = busGroup.DecodeFrame("Engine");
foreach (var signal in engineSignals)
{
    Console.WriteLine($"{signal.Key}: {signal.Value.ToArray().Average()}");
}

with meas.Reader("can_trace.meas") as reader:
    bus_group = reader["CAN1"]

    # Decode single signal
    rpm = bus_group.decode_signal("RPM")
    print(f"Average RPM: {rpm.mean()}")

    # Decode all signals in frame
    engine_signals = bus_group.decode_frame("Engine")
    for name, values in engine_signals.items():
        print(f"{name}: {values.mean()}")

docs.title: Raw Frame Wire Format
docs.text: Each bus type has a specific binary layout:

docs.title: CAN/CAN-FD
docs.text:
[uint32 arbId] [byte dlc] [byte flags] [payload]
  • arbId: 29-bit CAN identifier (extended format)
  • dlc: Data Length Code
  • flags: CAN-FD flags (BRS, ESI, etc.)

docs.title: LIN
docs.text:
[byte frameId] [byte dlc] [byte nad] [byte checksum] [payload]
  • frameId: LIN frame identifier
  • nad: Node Address
  • checksum: LIN checksum

docs.title: FlexRay
docs.text:
[uint16 slotId] [byte cycle] [byte flags] [uint16 len] [payload]
  • slotId: FlexRay slot identifier
  • cycle: Cycle counter
  • flags: Channel A/B, startup, etc.

docs.title: Ethernet
docs.text:
[6B macDst] [6B macSrc] [uint16 etherType] [uint16 vlan] [uint16 len] [payload]
  • macDst/macSrc: MAC addresses
  • etherType: Ethernet type/length
  • vlan: VLAN tag (optional)

docs.title: Automotive Test Automation
docs.text:
Record CAN traffic during vehicle tests:

// Record
using var writer = MeasFile.CreateWriter("drive_cycle.meas");
var can = writer.AddBusGroup("CAN_Powertrain", canConfig);
// ... define frames and signals
RecordTestDrive(can);

// Analyze
using var reader = MeasFile.OpenRead("drive_cycle.meas");
var rpm = reader["CAN_Powertrain"].DecodeSignal("RPM");
var maxRpm = rpm.ToArray().Max();
Console.WriteLine($"Max RPM: {maxRpm}");

docs.title: Signal Validation
docs.text:
Verify signal ranges and patterns:

var speed = busGroup.DecodeSignal("VehicleSpeed");
if (speed.ToArray().Any(s => s > 200))
{
    Console.WriteLine("Warning: Unrealistic speed detected");
}

docs.title: Cross-Language Analysis
docs.text:
Record with C, analyze with Python:

// Record in C
meas_writer_t* w = meas_writer_open("recording.meas");
// ... record CAN frames

# Analyze in Python
with meas.Reader("recording.meas") as reader:
    rpm = reader["CAN1"].decode_signal("RPM")
    plt.plot(rpm)
    plt.show()

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• Streaming Architecture
  • File Format Specification
  • API Reference
  • AUTOSAR Specifications

docs.title: File Format
docs.text: Complete binary format specification for .meas files.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Layer Content Size Header Magic, version, metadata 64 bytes (fixed) Metadata Segment Groups, channels, properties, bus definitions Variable Data Segments (repeated) Chunked channel data Variable

docs.title: File Header (64 bytes)
docs.text:
Offset | Size | Field              | Description
-------|------|--------------------|---------------------------------
0      | 5    | Magic              | "MEAS\0" (ASCII + null terminator)
5      | 1    | MajorVersion       | Format major version
6      | 1    | MinorVersion       | Format minor version
7      | 1    | Flags              | Header flags
8      | 8    | MetadataOffset     | Offset to first metadata segment
16     | 4    | SegmentCount       | Total number of data segments
20     | 4    | Reserved           | Reserved for future use
24     | 16   | FileGuid           | Unique file identifier (GUID)
40     | 8    | CreationTimestamp  | File creation time (Unix nanoseconds)
48     | 16   | Reserved           | Reserved for future use

docs.title: Magic Number
docs.text: MEAS\0 (0x4D 0x45 0x41 0x53 0x00) identifies the file format.

docs.title: Version
docs.text:
• Current: 1.0
  • MajorVersion: Breaking changes
  • MinorVersion: Backward-compatible additions

docs.title: Flags
docs.text: Bit Meaning 0 Extended metadata (file-level properties) 1-7 Reserved

docs.title: Metadata Segment
docs.text: Contains structural information and definitions.

docs.title: Segment Header
docs.text:
Offset | Size | Field        | Description
-------|------|--------------|---------------------------
0      | 8    | SegmentType  | 0x01 for metadata
8      | 8    | NextOffset   | Offset to next segment (0 if last)
16     | 8    | DataSize     | Size of segment data

docs.title: When Flags bit 0 is not set:
docs.text:
Offset | Size | Field              | Description
-------|------|--------------------|---------------------------
0      | 4    | GroupCount         | Number of groups
4      | ...  | Groups[]           | Group definitions

docs.title: When Flags bit 0 is set (extended metadata):
docs.text:
Offset | Size | Field              | Description
-------|------|--------------------|---------------------------
0      | 2    | MetadataMajor      | Metadata schema major version
2      | 2    | MetadataMinor      | Metadata schema minor version
4      | 4    | PropertyCount      | Number of file-level properties
8      | ...  | Properties[]       | File-level property key-value pairs
...    | 4    | GroupCount         | Number of groups
...    | ...  | Groups[]           | Group definitions

docs.title: Group Definition
docs.text:
Offset | Size | Field          | Description
-------|------|----------------|---------------------------
0      | 2    | NameLength     | Length of group name
2      | N    | Name           | UTF-8 group name
N+2    | 4    | ChannelCount   | Number of channels in group
N+6    | 4    | PropertyCount  | Number of group properties
N+10   | ...  | Properties[]   | Group properties
...    | ...  | Channels[]     | Channel definitions

docs.title: Channel Definition
docs.text:
Offset | Size | Field          | Description
-------|------|----------------|---------------------------
0      | 2    | NameLength     | Length of channel name
2      | N    | Name           | UTF-8 channel name
N+2    | 1    | DataType       | Data type code (see below)
N+3    | 8    | SampleCount    | Total number of samples
N+11   | 1    | HasStatistics  | 1 if statistics present, 0 otherwise
N+12   | 40   | Statistics     | Min/Max/Mean/StdDev (if HasStatistics=1)
...    | 4    | PropertyCount  | Number of channel properties
...    | ...  | Properties[]   | Channel properties

docs.title: Data Type Codes
docs.text: Code Type Size (bytes) 0x01 Int8 1 0x02 Int16 2 0x03 Int32 4 0x04 Int64 8 0x11 UInt8 1 0x12 UInt16 2 0x13 UInt32 4 0x14 UInt64 8 0x21 Float32 4 0x22 Float64 8 0x30 Bool 1 0x40 Timestamp 8 (nanoseconds) 0x50 Binary Variable

docs.title: Statistics Structure (40 bytes)
docs.text:
Offset | Size | Field    | Description
-------|------|----------|---------------------------
0      | 8    | Min      | Minimum value (float64)
8      | 8    | Max      | Maximum value (float64)
16     | 8    | Mean     | Average value (float64)
24     | 8    | StdDev   | Standard deviation (float64)
32     | 8    | Reserved | Reserved for future use

docs.title: Data Segment
docs.text: Contains actual measurement data.

docs.title: Segment Header
docs.text:
Offset | Size | Field        | Description
-------|------|--------------|---------------------------
0      | 8    | SegmentType  | 0x02 for data
8      | 8    | NextOffset   | Offset to next segment (0 if last)
16     | 8    | DataSize     | Total size of data in this segment

docs.title: Channel Data Chunks
docs.text: For each channel in order:

docs.title: Fixed-Size Types (Int, Float, Bool, Timestamp)
docs.text:
Offset | Size | Field          | Description
-------|------|----------------|---------------------------
0      | 4    | ChannelIndex   | Index of channel (0-based)
4      | 4    | SampleCount    | Number of samples in this chunk
8      | N×S  | Samples        | Raw sample data (N samples × S bytes each)

docs.title: Variable-Length Types (Binary)
docs.text:
Offset | Size | Field          | Description
-------|------|----------------|---------------------------
0      | 4    | ChannelIndex   | Index of channel (0-based)
4      | 4    | FrameCount     | Number of frames in this chunk
8      | ...  | Frames[]       | Length-prefixed frames

Each frame:

Offset | Size | Field          | Description
-------|------|----------------|---------------------------
0      | 4    | FrameLength    | Length of this frame
4      | N    | FrameData      | Raw frame bytes

docs.title: Compression (Optional)
docs.text: Data segments can be compressed:

docs.title: Compressed Segment Header
docs.text:
Offset | Size | Field              | Description
-------|------|--------------------|---------------------------
0      | 8    | SegmentType        | 0x82 for compressed data
8      | 8    | NextOffset         | Offset to next segment
16     | 8    | CompressedSize     | Size of compressed data
24     | 8    | UncompressedSize   | Original data size
32     | 1    | CompressionType    | 0x01=LZ4, 0x02=Zstd
33     | ...  | CompressedData     | Compressed segment data

docs.title: Compression Types
docs.text: Code Algorithm Typical Ratio 0x01 LZ4 2-3× (fast) 0x02 Zstd 3-5× (better compression)

docs.title: Endianness
docs.text: All multi-byte integers are little-endian.

docs.title: Alignment
docs.text: No special alignment requirements. Fields are packed sequentially.

docs.title: Required Checks
docs.text:
1. Magic number: Must be MEAS\0
  2. Version: Major version must match implementation
  3. Segment chain: All NextOffset values must be valid or 0
  4. Sample counts: Sum of chunk sample counts must equal channel SampleCount

docs.title: Recommended Checks
docs.text:
1. CRC/Checksum: Not currently included (future addition)
  2. GUID uniqueness: Verify file GUID is unique
  3. Timestamp ordering: Verify timestamps are monotonically increasing

docs.title: Level 1: Basic
docs.text:
• Read/write fixed-size channels (Int, Float)
  • Read metadata
  • No compression support

docs.title: Level 2: Extended
docs.text:
• Variable-length channels (Binary for bus data)
  • Statistics computation
  • Properties

docs.title: Level 3: Full
docs.text:
• All data types
  • Compression (LZ4, Zstd)
  • Bus data definitions (CAN, LIN, etc.)
  • E2E protection, SecOC

docs.title: Future Extensions
docs.text:
Reserved fields allow for backward-compatible additions:

  • File-level encryption
  • Digital signatures
  • Embedded thumbnails/previews
  • Advanced compression codecs

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• Streaming Architecture
  • Bus Data Support
  • Full Specification (GitHub)

docs.title: Streaming Architecture
docs.text: MeasFlow is streaming-first — the format is designed so data can be written and read incrementally without holding the entire file in memory.

docs.title: Design Principles
docs.text:
1. Bounded Memory: Memory usage stays constant regardless of file size
  2. Forward-Only: No seeking backwards required during write
  3. Incremental Flush: Data segments written as they're ready
  4. Readable During Write: Partial files can be read while writing continues

docs.title: File Layout
docs.text:
MeasFlow uses a segment-linked file layout:

┌───────────────────┐
│  File Header 64B  │  Magic: MEAS\0, Version, GUID, Timestamp
├───────────────────┤
│  Metadata Segment │  Groups, channels, properties, bus definitions
│  → NextOffset ────┼──┐
├───────────────────┤  │
│  Data Segment #1  │◄─┘  First Flush() result
│  → NextOffset ────┼──┐
├───────────────────┤  │
│  Data Segment #2  │◄─┘  Second Flush() result
│  → NextOffset ────┼──┐
├───────────────────┤  │
│  Data Segment #N  │◄─┘  Final Dispose() data
└───────────────────┘

docs.title: Segment Linking
docs.text:
• Segments form a forward-linked list
  • Each segment contains a NextOffset pointer to the next segment
  • Readers scan sequentially—no random access needed
  • Last segment has NextOffset = 0

docs.title: Streaming Writes
docs.text: Writers maintain a single in-memory buffer that gets flushed to disk periodically.

docs.title: Write Workflow
docs.text:
1. Application writes data to channels
  2. Data accumulates in memory buffer
  3. Application calls Flush()
  4. Buffer written as new data segment
  5. Buffer cleared
  6. Repeat from step 1

docs.title: Memory Usage
docs.text:
Memory usage is independent of recording duration:

C#PythonC 
using var writer = MeasFile.CreateWriter("long_recording.meas");
var group = writer.AddGroup("Sensors");
var temp = group.AddChannel<float>("Temperature");

// Record for hours—memory stays constant
while (recording)
{
    float[] batch = ReadFromSensor(batchSize: 10_000);
    temp.Write(batch.AsSpan());
    writer.Flush();  // Memory cleared here
}

with meas.Writer("long_recording.meas") as writer:
    group = writer.add_group("Sensors")
    temp = group.add_channel("Temperature", meas.DataType.Float32)

    # Record for hours—memory stays constant
    while recording:
        batch = read_from_sensor(batch_size=10_000)
        temp.write(batch)
        writer.flush()  # Memory cleared here

meas_writer_t* w = meas_writer_open("long_recording.meas");
meas_group_t* g = meas_writer_add_group(w, "Sensors");
meas_channel_t* temp = meas_group_add_channel(g, "Temperature", MEAS_FLOAT32);

// Record for hours—memory stays constant
while (recording) {
    float batch[10000];
    read_from_sensor(batch, 10000);
    meas_channel_write_f32(temp, batch, 10000);
    meas_writer_flush(w);  // Memory cleared here
}
meas_writer_close(w);

docs.title: Streaming Reads
docs.text: Readers can process files chunk-by-chunk without loading the entire dataset.

docs.title: Chunk-Based Reading
Instant Statistics
docs.text:
Statistics are computed during write and stored in metadata. Reading statistics requires zero I/O:

C#PythonC 
using var reader = MeasFile.OpenRead("data.meas");
var channel = reader["Sensors"]["Temperature"];

var stats = channel.Statistics;  // No data read!
Console.WriteLine($"Range: {stats?.Min} - {stats?.Max}");
Console.WriteLine($"Mean: {stats?.Mean}, StdDev: {stats?.StdDev}");

with meas.Reader("data.meas") as reader:
    channel = reader["Sensors"]["Temperature"]

    stats = channel.statistics  # No data read!
    print(f"Range: {stats.min} - {stats.max}")
    print(f"Mean: {stats.mean}, StdDev: {stats.std_dev}")

const meas_channel_data_t* ch = /* ... */;

if (ch->has_stats) {  // No data read!
    printf("Range: %.2f - %.2f\n", ch->stats.min, ch->stats.max);
    printf("Mean: %.2f, StdDev: %.2f\n", ch->stats.mean, ch->stats.std_dev);
}

docs.title: Live File Reading
docs.text: Partial files (writer still open) can be read up to the last complete segment.

docs.title: Use Case: Real-Time Monitoring
docs.text:
Writer process:

// Process A: Writing
using var writer = MeasFile.CreateWriter("live.meas");
var temp = writer.AddGroup("Sensors").AddChannel<float>("Temperature");

while (true)
{
    temp.Write(ReadSensor());
    writer.Flush();  // Segment immediately available
    Thread.Sleep(100);
}

Reader process (parallel):

// Process B: Reading (while A is still writing)
using var reader = MeasFile.OpenRead("live.meas");
var channel = reader["Sensors"]["Temperature"];

// Reads all data written so far (up to last flush)
var data = channel.ReadAll<float>();

docs.title: Write Performance
docs.text:
• Throughput: ~500 MB/s uncompressed (SSD)
  • Memory: Constant (configurable buffer size)
  • Latency: Flush completes in ~10ms for 1MB buffer

docs.title: Read Performance
docs.text:
• Sequential read: ~1 GB/s (memory-mapped I/O)
  • Statistics read: ~1 μs (metadata only)
  • Chunk iteration: Zero-copy, minimal overhead

docs.title: Comparison with Other Formats
docs.text: Format Streaming Write Streaming Read Memory Usage MeasFlow ✅ Native ✅ Native Constant HDF5 ⚠️ Requires chunking ✅ With chunked storage Variable TDMS ✅ Native ⚠️ Limited Variable MDF4 ❌ Finalize required ⚠️ Complex High

docs.title: When to Flush
docs.text:
• Time-based: Flush every N seconds (e.g., 1-10s)
  • Size-based: Flush when buffer reaches N MB (e.g., 10-100 MB)
  • Event-based: Flush on significant events (test completion, error, etc.)

docs.title: Chunk Size Selection
docs.text:
• Small files (< 100 MB): Flush less frequently (larger chunks)
  • Large files (> 1 GB): Flush more frequently (smaller chunks)
  • Real-time monitoring: Flush frequently (small chunks, low latency)

docs.title: Reading Strategies
docs.text:
• Full analysis: Use ReadAll() if file fits in memory
  • Large files: Use ReadChunks() for streaming processing
  • Preview: Read statistics only, then read data if needed

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• File Format Specification
  • Bus Data Support
  • API Reference
